checkpoint.summary: unit_046 complete. phase-contract.md Units of Work table gained 3 new rows (depends_on, parallel_safe, touches_paths) + a 'Required' column that makes the when-required conditional explicit. The touches_paths conditional ('required when parallel_safe: true') is the most error-prone -- the new row both pins it and cross-links to the Scope-Violation Enforcement Policy section so readers chase the trust-boundary rationale. Also added a new top-level 'Decomposing a phase for parallelism' section after Scope-Violation Enforcement Policy. Five numbered steps walk phase authors through the recipe: dependency graph authoring (distinguish real depends_on from logical-ordering vibes), blast radius identification (one-directory-per-unit heuristic + shared-utilities warning), overlap check (fnmatch-based overlap matrix + 'narrow globs, don't disable the check'), parallel_safe as 'declaration of independence, not performance hint' with 4 criteria, dry-run via frontier-piped-into-compute. Closes with Anti-patterns subsection naming 3 real traps. New test_phase_contract_parallelism_fields.py (170 LOC, 11 cases across 3 classes) pins the table additions + all 5 Step sub-headings + anti-patterns subsection + position-order between the Scope-Violation policy and the decomposition section. Suite 254 -> 265.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_047 (PHASE_011): create new skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md covering the dispatch lifecycle, overlap-matrix algorithm details, merge-order rationale, failure-mode catalog, recovery procedures, and a readiness checklist. This is the deep-dive companion to architecture.md's new Parallel Execution Model summary section -- architecture.md links forward to this doc. Much of the content already exists in the code's module docstrings + phase-graph.json validation_evidence fields; this unit consolidates it into a single readable reference. Validation: grep for major section headings + structural presence test similar to unit_045/unit_046.
